product: Attribute-based access control (ABAC) in Unity Catalog is in Public Preview
tagline: Attribute based access control (ABAC) for Unity Catalog is now in Public Preview.
what_it_does: Attribute based access control (ABAC) for Unity Catalog is now in Public Preview. ABAC lets you govern access to data using policies that reference attributes — tags on securable objects and properties of the requesting principal — instead of granting permissions object by object. With ABAC you can: Write policies that apply automatically to any table tagged with a classification such as pii or restricted.
why_it_matters: ABAC lets you govern access to data using policies that reference attributes — tags on securable objects and properties of the requesting principal — instead of granting permissions object by object.
plain_summary: Attribute based access control (ABAC) for Unity Catalog is now in Public Preview. ABAC lets you govern access to data using policies that reference attributes — tags on securable objects and properties of the requesting principal — instead of granting…

capabilities.title: Write policies that apply automatically
capabilities.desc: Write policies that apply automatically to any table tagged with a classification such as pii or restricted.

capabilities.title: Enforce column masking and row
capabilities.desc: Enforce column masking and row filtering based on principal attributes.

capabilities.title: Centralize governance so new tables
capabilities.desc: Centralize governance so new tables inherit the right controls on creation.

prerequisites: Tag-based governance enabled for the metastore.
limitations: Policy evaluation currently supports a subset of SQL predicate functions.
ABAC policies are not yet enforced for Delta Sharing recipients.
key_takeaway: Attribute based access control (ABAC) for Unity Catalog is now in Public Preview.
